title: Configuration Management and Delivery Public Checklist

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.16, does your Software Configuration Index:
records.query: Identify exactly what makes up the software — every component, version, and part number?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.16, does your Software Configuration Index:
records.query: Identify the tools, settings, and environment needed to rebuild the software from scratch?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.16, does your Software Configuration Index:
records.query: List the life-cycle data and its versions, so the build is fully reproducible?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7, in your day-to-day configuration management:
records.query: Is everything under control uniquely identified, with changes recorded and traceable to problem reports?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7, in your day-to-day configuration management:
records.query: Can you reproduce a released build exactly from the controlled data?

records.subsection: Based on FAA software conformity and first-article-inspection expectations:
records.query: Have you confirmed the software actually loaded on the hardware matches the released part number and version (a conformity check)?

records.subsection: Based on FAA software conformity and first-article-inspection expectations:
records.query: Is the delivered media correctly labeled and formatted, with the right part numbers?

records.subsection: Based on FAA software conformity and first-article-inspection expectations:
records.query: Do you have the acceptance records showing the article was inspected and accepted?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7.2.7 and §11.16, when you deliver:
records.query: Do you deliver the controlled version together with its configuration index and the instructions to reproduce it?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7.2.7 and §11.16, when you deliver:
records.query: For pre-production or engineering releases, is the release status clearly marked, so a development build is not mistaken for a certified one?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available through donations. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
